# Getting started

Welcome. This guide takes you from install to your first reactive template binding.

`@rx-angular/template` gives you directives and pipes for high performance rendering that never blocks the main thread. You import only what you need from independent entry points like `@rx-angular/template/let`, `@rx-angular/template/for`, and `@rx-angular/template/push`.

## Before you start

This guide assumes you are comfortable with Angular standalone components. It also helps to know the basics of RxJS observables.

## Install

Install the package with your package manager.

```sh
npm install @rx-angular/template
```

You can also use yarn.

```sh
yarn add @rx-angular/template
```

## Your first binding with `*rxLet`

`*rxLet` subscribes to an observable for you and renders its latest value. You get this without the `async` pipe and without relying on `NgZone`.

Add `RxLet` to a standalone component and bind an observable in the template.

```ts
import { Component } from '@angular/core';
import { RxLet } from '@rx-angular/template/let';
import { interval, map } from 'rxjs';

@Component({
  selector: 'app-clock',
  imports: [RxLet],
  template: `
    <ng-container *rxLet="time$; let time">
      {{ time }}
    </ng-container>
  `,
})
export class ClockComponent {
  readonly time$ = interval(1000).pipe(map(() => new Date().toLocaleTimeString()));
}
```

Run the app. You will see the time update every second. `*rxLet` did the subscription and the rendering for you.

Pick the path that fits what you need.

- Solve a specific task in the [Guides](./guides/).
- Understand the ideas behind the directives in the [Concepts](/docs/concepts).
